description: Shared game-data manifest for DataByteSprites: Data Discovery. This manifest connects public roster data, Studio species metadata, moves, abilities, type rules, item definitions, encounter pools, and future asset bindings into one discoverable source for the game and diagnostics.

items.name: ByteCoin
items.description: Spent to attempt capturing a wild signal.

items.name: Signal Boost
items.description: Improves the capture window during battle.

items.name: Repair Pulse
items.description: Future healing item for restoring lead sprite health.

encounterPools.label: Common Signal
encounterPools.description: Default scanner results for low-noise codes.

encounterPools.label: Rare Signal
encounterPools.description: Higher-quality scanner result for stronger codes.

encounterPools.label: Deep Signal
encounterPools.description: Rare deep-signal hit reserved for special codes and future story hooks.

mergeRules.lockedRecords: Do not include locked Studio records in normal capture pools unless a future unlock rule explicitly allows them.

validationTargets: Every playable roster entry should have dex, name, type, stage, rarity, icon, lore, capture chance, and stability.
Every Studio-backed playable match should expose studioId and studioSource.
Moves should reference known species IDs before they are enabled in battle.
Abilities should reference known species IDs before they are enabled in battle.
Type chart rules should reference known type names before type effectiveness is enabled.
Items should have id, name, type, and startingQuantity.
Encounter pools should have id, label, weight, and rarity filters.
DataByteDex and Data Discovery should consume the same runtime roster global.
